Primary customers: Mass-market consumers, sold via SodaStream's own retail/e-commerce channels and third-party retailers (supermarkets, department stores, e-commerce) in 40+ countries.

Core model: SodaStream sells a B2C razor-and-blades model: consumers buy a home carbonation machine and reusable CO2 cylinders, then return empty cylinders for exchange (via SodaStream's own retail/online exchange program or third-party retail exchange points) rather than buying new ones — each returned cylinder is inspected, sterilized and refilled through what the company calls its "Triple Quality Control" process before re-entering circulation. This cylinder-exchange loop is the company's core reuse mechanism and displaces both single-use beverage packaging (bottles/cans) and single-use gas canisters.
Key partners / customers: - PepsiCo, Inc. — parent company since August 2018 (source: Wikipedia; company press materials) - Enlight Renewable Energy — renewable-energy supply partnership for production sites (source: PR Newswire, 2024) - TerraCycle — US recycling program partnership announced April 2024 (source: PR Newswire, 2024)
Impact metrics: - Company states that in 2023, beverages consumed via SodaStream were equivalent to avoiding approximately 5.5 billion single-use plastic bottles (source: PR Newswire press release, 2024, "SodaStream Champions Sustainability"). Self-reported company figure; no independent audit identified. - Employee headcount reported at approximately 1,907 (2024) and 1,981 (2023) by third-party workforce-analytics provider Revelio Labs — not an official company disclosure; treat as approximate.

Primary customers: Events, municipal/public-sector entities, universities, and office/tertiary-sector organisations in France.
Ownership status: Privately held; ownership not disclosed publicly
Core model: Wobz rents out shared, mutualised reusable containers (cups, deposit mats, connected terminals) tracked via its MagicLoop® digital deposit system, and provides the high-performance washing infrastructure needed to run a closed reuse loop, along with customisation and R&D services for scaling reuse deployments. Clients pay for container rental/service rather than owning the reusable assets themselves, aligning with a packaging-as-a-service model. It is a member of the Vrac & Réemploi Network and participates in the "New ERA" and CEC circular-economy movements.
Key partners / customers: Vrac & Réemploi Network (member); New ERA movement; CEC (circular-economy) pathway; related entity Wobz Print Solutions.
Impact metrics: Company site references 2024 figures for partner events hosted, shared containers deployed, and estimated tonnes of waste avoided, but exact values were not retrievable from the page content reviewed (shown as placeholders in the fetched source).

Primary customers: B2B packaging producers and food/retail companies, including Sodexo and other branded partners; used in fresh-produce packaging (e.g., with HKScan in Finland).

Core model: Woodly is a B2B materials supplier, licensing/selling its Woodly® wood-cellulose bioplastic as films, flexibles, and rigid formats to packaging producers and food companies (e.g., Sodexo among branded partners) as a substitute for conventional fossil-based plastic in fresh-food packaging. The material is marketed as mechanically recyclable and free of PFAS, BPA, phthalates, and CMR chemicals, extending shelf life and supporting customers' packaging-sustainability goals; Woodly earned RECOUP certification (June 2026) confirming it is collectable, detectable, and mechanically recyclable.
Geographic scope: Finland (Helsinki); UK commercial office also referenced for London/UK growth
Key partners / customers: VTT (Finland's state research institute, material co-development); Sodexo; HKScan; RECOUP (UK recycling certification body).
Impact metrics: RECOUP-certified (June 2026) as collectable, detectable, and mechanically recyclable; company states the material extends fresh-produce shelf life and reduces food waste/CO2e, though no independently audited tonnage figures were found.
